title: Saltugilia splendens
taxonomy: Saltugilia splendens is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae. This family is commonly known as the "gloam-pink, or skyrocket" family. Saltugilia splendens is native to western North America and is a member of the genus Saltugilia, which is a group of flowering plants that are closely related to the genus Gilia. The exact classification of Saltugilia splendens can be further detailed to include its rank as a species within the genus, which is recognized as Saltugilia splendens Greene. It is also known to be a part of the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Saltugilia splendens, also known as Western wallflower or Western tower of jewels, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. intro: Saltugilia splendens, also known as a woodland phlox, is a flowering plant species native to the western United States. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects that may help to alleviate respiratory issues and wounds. Bioactive constituents of Saltugilia splendens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties.
